name: Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353)
name_original: Monumento Natural Fuente de los Cien Caños – trail run circular a la Sierra de San Jorge (PR-A 339)
description: This difficult 8.1-mile (13.0 km) route in Sierra de San Jorge offers rugged terrain, karstic landscapes, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Villanueva Del Trabuco offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its mountainous terrain, including the Sierra de San Jorge, and forested areas like the Forest of Las Morillas. The region features varied elevations and natural gorges, providing a range of running experiences. There are 36 running routes available, catering to different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Villanueva Del Trabuco The most popular jogging route is Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353), an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route offers challenging ascents through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268), a moderate 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This running route winds through a scenic forest landscape. Local runners also love the Impressive Gorge loop from Villanueva del Rosario, an 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail leading through a notable gorge, often completed in about 5 hours. Jogging around Villanueva Del Trabuco is defined by its mountain trails, forested paths, and impressive gorges. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The routes in Villanueva Del Trabuco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Trabuco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: Villanueva Del Trabuco offers a wide selection of running routes, with &lt;strong&gt;over 35 trails&lt;/strong&gt; to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate to challenging.

Q: Are there challenging running trails in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: Yes, Villanueva Del Trabuco is known for its challenging terrain. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;20 difficult running routes&lt;/strong&gt;, often featuring significant elevation changes through mountainous areas like the Sierra de San Jorge. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996008048/sierra-de-san-jorge-trail-pr-a-353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353)&lt;/a&gt;, which includes demanding ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: The region's running routes are characterized by diverse terrain, including mountainous paths, forested areas like the Forest of Las Morillas, and impressive natural gorges. You'll encounter varied elevations, providing a dynamic experience for runners.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in the area?
A: For those seeking longer runs, Villanueva Del Trabuco offers several extended options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31436323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Impressive Gorge loop from Villanueva del Rosario&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, covering nearly 18.5 miles (29.8 km) and typically taking around 5 hours to complete.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: The running routes in Villanueva Del Trabuco are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;3.3 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from more than 5 reviews. Over 250 runners have used komoot to explore the varied and scenic terrain here.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through a beautiful forest landscape.

Q: What are some good running routes for beginners or those looking for a moderate challenge?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are &lt;strong&gt;16 moderate running routes&lt;/strong&gt; available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996265302/forest-of-las-morillas-circular-from-alfarnate-pr-a-268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of Las Morillas – circular from Alfarnate (PR-A 268)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6-mile path, perfect for those seeking a less strenuous but still scenic run.

Q: What natural attractions can I see near the running routes?
A: The region boasts several natural attractions that can be explored. You might encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Vilo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;, or the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1116374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafarraya Plain&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4597974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Frío Springs&lt;/a&gt; also offer a beautiful natural sight.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or landmarks along the trails?
A: Yes, runners can enjoy various viewpoints and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gomer and Doña Ana cliffs&lt;/a&gt; provides stunning vistas. Additionally, mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/862950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Sol (1085 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Sábar&lt;/a&gt; are notable points of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Villanueva Del Trabuco?
A: Given its mountainous terrain and Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable conditions for running in Villanueva Del Trabuco, with milder temperatures. Summer can be quite warm, especially at midday, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable during those months.

Q: Is there a running route that explores the Peñón de los Becerros area?
A: Yes, you can explore the Peñón de los Becerros area with a dedicated running route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de los Becerros loop from Villanueva del Rosario&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.3-mile (18.2 km) trail that takes you through this scenic part of the region.

Q: Are there any shorter, moderate running loops available?
A: For a shorter, moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31440840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Repsol&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers about 5.9 miles (9.5 km) and offers a moderate challenge, making it suitable for a quicker run.
tips: The lagoon is located in a large sinkhole, a karstic depression that acts as a rainwater collector. The lagoon can be reached via the Sierra de San Jorge Trail PR-A 353, which starts from the Fuente de los Cien Caños.

The Fuente de los Cien Caños is an impressive natural monument located in Villanueva del Trabuco, on the limestone slope of the Sierra de San Jorge. The fountain is known for being the source of the Guadalhorce River, the longest and most voluminous river in the province of Malaga.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353) suitable for jogging?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally not suitable for typical jogging. It is officially rated as 'Alta' (High difficulty) and features rugged, unpaved terrain with sharp limestone formations (lapiaz). The path condition is described as 'malo' (bad), often requiring careful footing, and even the use of hands or jumps to maintain balance. It's more appropriate for experienced hikers or trail runners accustomed to very technical and challenging conditions.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly rugged and unpaved, characterized by extensive 'lapiaz' – a landscape of sharp, pointed limestone formations and stones. While some sections pass through reforested pine forests and dirt tracks, a significant portion involves navigating over rocks and uneven ground. The path condition is generally poor, demanding careful attention to footing.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends at the Hundred-Spout Fountain (Fuente de los 100 Caños) in Villanueva del Trabuco. Parking is typically available near this natural monument, which serves as the main access point for the trail.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the route?
faqs.answer: Given its high difficulty and challenging terrain, the estimated time to complete the route by foot is around 5 hours. This accounts for the significant ascent and the need for careful navigation over uneven ground.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful and singular views. You'll experience panoramic vistas of the Guadalhorce river basin and the surrounding mountain ranges. From the crest of the sierra, views extend over the Axarquía region and towards the Pico de la Maroma. Notable features include the unique karstic morphology with dolines and uvalas, and the picturesque Laguna de San Jorge, an oasis amidst the stony landscape.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to hike this trail, nor is there an entrance fee. It is an officially approved (homologated) trail by the Andalusian Mountain Sports Federation (FAM) and traverses the LIC (Place of Community Interest) Sierra de Camarolos, which is a protected area, but access is generally open.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on public trails in Spain, given the 'Alta' difficulty, rugged terrain, and sharp limestone formations, this trail can be very challenging for dogs, especially those not accustomed to such conditions. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially as it traverses a protected area (LIC Sierra de Camarolos), and ensure they are physically capable of handling the demanding route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically during the cooler months of spring and autumn. Summers can be very hot, making the strenuous climb and exposed sections more challenging. Winter can also be suitable, but be prepared for potentially colder temperatures and occasional wet conditions which could make the already difficult terrain even more slippery.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially overlaps with other trails. You will find sections that coincide with the Sierra de San Jorge trail (83% of the route), Los Caños (32%), and Camino de los Poyales (22%). Smaller portions also intersect with Sierra Gorda, Andalucía - North Route (Provincia de Málaga), Alfarnate - Villanueva del Rosario, Camino del Cortijo Eulogio, and Camino de la Moheda.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this challenging trail?
faqs.answer: Given the 'Alta' difficulty and rugged terrain, it's crucial to be well-prepared. We recommend sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can be very helpful for stability on the uneven ground. Due to the karstic landscape, be aware of potential sharp rocks and consider durable clothing.

faqs.question: Is the route a circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierra de San Jorge Trail (PR-A 353) is a circular route, starting and ending at the Hundred-Spout Fountain.
